summary.intent: Rework the BranchLog view to use the same two-panel layout as SessionDetail (checkpoints on the left, session chat on the right), with each checkpoint showing only its own events.
summary.outcome: BranchLog.tsx was rewritten with the two-panel layout. A bug where checkpoints sharing the same checkpointId across different sessions were co-selected was fixed by switching the selection key to a composite `${sessionId}:${checkpointId}` format.

repo: BranchLogEntry items can share the same checkpointId across different sessions — using checkpointId alone as a selection/grouping key causes multiple unrelated checkpoints to be treated as one.
Session log events must be bucketed by checkpoint timestamp windows (comparing event.timestamp against checkpoint.createdAt) to show only events belonging to a specific checkpoint.
The CheckpointRow component is shared between SessionDetail.tsx and BranchLog.tsx via import from SessionDetail.
Session log events are fetched per-session and should be cached to avoid redundant fetches when switching between checkpoints in the same session.

code.finding: Selection key must be `${sessionId}:${checkpointId}` (composite) not just `checkpointId`, because multiple BranchLogEntry rows from different sessions can share the same checkpointId value.

buildCheckpointGroups helper buckets log events by timestamp: each event is assigned to the first checkpoint whose createdAt >= event.timestamp; events after the last checkpoint go into the 'shadow' bucket.

workflow: When extracting a pattern from one screen to another, verify that the assumptions baked into the original (e.g., unique checkpointIds) hold in the new context before reusing the logic verbatim.
Caching fetched session events in a Map keyed by sessionId prevents redundant API calls when toggling between checkpoints of the same session.

friction: The initial BranchLog rewrite used checkpointId as the selection key, which caused multiple checkpoints across different sessions to be selected simultaneously — required a second debugging pass to identify and fix.
The composite key fix required touching multiple Edit calls across the same file (selectedId comparisons, click handler, cache key) due to the key being used in several places.
